question_text: A student observes that water evaporates from a puddle over several days. Which statement correctly describes this using particle theory?

options.label: A
options.text: Water particles at the surface gain energy and escape into the air as individual particles

options.label: B
options.text: All water particles turn into ice before becoming a gas

options.label: C
options.text: Water particles break apart into hydrogen and oxygen atoms

options.label: D
options.text: The water disappears because the particles are too small to see

explanation: Evaporation occurs when particles at the liquid surface gain enough energy to overcome attractive forces and escape as gas particles. This is a physical change, not a chemical one, and particles remain intact as water molecules throughout the process.
